scope_note: QA Music of the Spheres Scale cert [324]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1995) Quantum Arithmetic Book 3 (QA-3), ITAM Portland, ISBN 1-883401-08-9, Chapter 6: 'THE MYRIAD OF SOUND'. Five structural results certified: (C1) 8 keynotes = 8 Satellite states: mod-9 Satellite orbit has exactly 8 states {(b,b): b in {1..8}}, matching 8 I-Ching entities (4 male + 4 female) from the Cattle Problem framework (Iverson p.81). (C2) 17 reduced fractions: exactly 17 fractions p/q with gcd(p,q)=1, 1<=p<q<=7 exist in (0,1); adding the keynote itself gives 18 notes per key (Iverson p.82: 'eighteen low fractional values'). (C3) 144 = 8 * 18: 8 keynotes * 18 notes = 144 total notes across all keys (Iverson p.82: '144 different notes in eight keys'). (C4) 7-smooth denominators: all 17 scale fractions have denominators with prime factors in {2,3,5,7} only -- confirms Iverson's 'harmony depends on fractional relationships from halves to sevenths' (p.91). (C5) Farey mediant property: the 17 sorted scale fractions satisfy |p1*q2 - p2*q1| = 1 at all 16 adjacent pairs, making them Farey neighbors -- the strongest harmonic adjacency condition. Builds on certs [322] (harmonic aliquot), [323] (LCM chemistry). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
